observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MA to submit the DVs and corresponding supporting documents for the transactions totaling to ₱23,590,814.97 for post-audit to preclude the issuance of a notice of suspension on the audit of subject transactions.
We recommended that the LCE require the MAO, Appraisal Committee, and Municipal Assessor to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the payment on procured parcel of land.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to support the procurements made from various suppliers and henceforth comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the BAC and MA to submit the documentary requirements on the emergency procurement made from various suppliers.

We recommended that the LCE

(a) activate and strengthen the GFPS in accordance with the afore-cited rules and regulations to ensure the mainstreaming of GAD in the systems and processes of the Municipality and 

(b) require the GFPS to 

(i) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs to ensure is not based on incorrect assumption and stereotypes; 

(ii) update the Municipality’s GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines; and 

(iii) initiate the capacitation of its members on the use of HGDG tool to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MA and MT to conduct regular reconciliation of their cash records to immediately address the deficiencies thereof; (b) record and adjust immediately all unrecorded transactions and reconciling items found in their respective records; and (c) MA to (i) record disbursements in the CKDJ based on the Report of Checks Issued to limit the occurrence of unreleased checks recognized as disbursements at year-end; (ii) make the necessary adjustments for the stale/cancelled checks; and (iii) review the BRS for the unrecorded debit/credit memos and make representation with the bank for errors found.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the MA to (i) ensure strict compliance with the PIM in the recording of inventories upon purchase; and (ii) make the necessary adjustments in the books to properly recognize inventories on-hand at year-end and recognize as expense the distributed stocks accordingly; and (b) instruct the designated GSO and MA to (i) reconcile inventory records; and (ii) establish an  Inventory Management process indicating the required documents to be prepared upon receipt/delivery, issuance, and at month-end, including the deadline of submission of reports, and to whom the reports are submitted as prescribed by  the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I to ensure accuracy of records, promote operational efficiency, and encourage adherence to policies, rules, regulations, and laws.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) authorize the PMT to be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed; and (b) require the PMT to (i) prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the specific assignment/duties of the members, cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the target completion date of the physical inventory; (ii) exhaust all diligent efforts to establish the non-existing/missing PPE; (iii) conduct an investigation to determine the last known location/office where the missing PPE were issued/installed, person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others; and (iv) refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and take immediate steps in accounting and derecognition of  non-existing/missing PPE to address the issue on the fairness of presentation of the financial position of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Engineer (ME) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to improve the system of  identifying  and implementing priority PPAs  to provide  timely socio-economic benefits to its constituents; (b) BAC to (i) submit copies of contracts, POs, and other documents required under the alternative modes of procurement; (ii) comply with GPPB  Circular No. 18-2020 on the conduct of procurement activities under RA No. 11949 or the Bayanihan 2 particularly the posting of ABC, contract or PO, Notice of Award (NOA), and other relevant documents to the GPPB Online Portal, Municipality’s website, or at any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose; (iii) subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are met for the use of alternative mode of procurement; and (c) MA to (i) submit the DV and SD for the procurement of vaccines, parcel of lot and motor vehicle with transaction values of ₱3,842,400.00, ₱1,000,000.00 and ₱1,492,500.00; and (ii)  ensure that the necessary supporting documents attached to DVs are complete and in order prior to payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) submit on a monthly basis the report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF based on the required format prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; (b) ensure that supporting documents are complete and in order before payment to contractors and suppliers is made; and (c) recognize all types of inventories as asset upon purchase and recognize the used portion as expense upon submission of signed issuance documents to the MAO.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MA to (i) submit to the Audit Team the DVs and SDs for the transactions totaling  ₱8,442,871.31 for post-audit; and (b) BAC to (i) send notices in writing  to at least three observers before each procurement activity; (ii) post the advertisement, notices of award and approved contract at the PhilGEPS and at prescribed places; and (iii) require from the prospective bidders the submission of NFCC , SLCC and  Statement of Completed and On-going Private and Government Contracts; and (c) ME to submit the lacking supporting documents for progress and final billing particularly the as -built plans and test results of construction materials.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to  require the MPDC, MA  and implementing offices to (a) implement  only PPAs under the LGSF that are included in the AIP of the LDRRM; and (b) submit the periodic reports on the utilization of the LGSF, the proof of posting thereof to the Municipality’s website, and in at least three conspicuous places and comply with other reporting requirements as required under DBM LBC Nos. 130 and 134.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA, in coordination with other concerned Municipal Offices to (a) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds for the unused balances amounting to ₱1,151,309.88 lodged to the Due to NGAs accounts, otherwise, revert the amount to the National Treasury; and (b) prepare SL for each fund transfers and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of fund transfers to source NGAs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the BAC to require from the prospective bidders the submission of updated eligibility documents, statement of all its ongoing government and private contracts, including contracts awarded but not yet started, latest financial statements stamped received by the BIR, SLCC and NFCC; (b) LSB to support the payment of (i) self-learning modules with DepEd guidelines or memorandum that the same was not funded by the DepEd; and (ii) textbooks and supplementary materials/workbooks with written report submitted to the OSEC and BLR before the start of procurement justifying the necessity to procure the textbooks; and (c)  the MA to (i) submit the complete DVs and SDs to support claims against the SEF budget including the distribution lists for the procured textbooks and supplementary materials/workbooks aggregating to ₱17,482,596.80 and school supplies amounting to ₱2,975,425.40; and (ii) recognize the acquisition of textbooks and similar materials in accordance with the prescribed Chart of Accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to verify and establish supporting documents for the adjustments made totalling to ₱306,315.88 and to establish a control mechanism to validate system generated reports to ensure reliability of data.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) HRMO to submit in a timely manner the required payroll documents to ensure timely processing and recording of transactions in the books, and (b) continue to withheld and remit intact premiums and contributions to the GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG pursuant to relevant laws.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System to (a) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes; (b) initiate the capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to guarantee the integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the agency programs and projects and to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and (c) create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a)  Municipal Accountant (MA) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to conduct regular reconciliation of their cash records to immediately address the deficiencies thereof; and 
(b) MA to 

(i) record and adjust immediately all unrecorded transactions and reconciling items found in their respective records; 

(ii) prepare bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis to prevent the accumulation of reconciling items that require adjustment in the books to report a reliable Cash-in-Bank account balance; and 

(iii) recognize in the books the unaccounted five bank accounts based on bank confirmation reply.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to 

(i) adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of inventories; 
(ii) recognize in the books the unused inventories amounting to ₱1,189,164.93; and 

(iii) maintain SLCs to record the acquisition, issuance and disposal of inventories; and 

(b) Property Officer to prepare the RSMI monthly supported with the RIS for the preparation of Journal Voucher to recognize the issued inventories for the month pursuant to the pertinent Sections of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the LCE require the PMT and MAO to 

(i) complete the physical count of all PPE; 

(ii) prepare the required PCs and PPELCs which shall contain the details of each PPE in the inventory control account in the general ledger; and 

(iii) reconcile their respective property records regularly for fair presentation of each type of PPE.

We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) MA and MICTO to reconcile their records to ensure that all Intangible Assets particularly application systems, software, databases and the likes are properly recognized in the books; and 
(b) MA to make the necessary adjustments and recognize other Intangible Assets previously overlooked in the books.

We recommended that the LCE  

(a) create a committee composed of the MA, MTO, MICTO and other concerned Offices/Officers to analyze and make recommendations to address the identified weaknesses in the information technology system; and 

(b) require the System Provider to make over-all improvements in the System on the aspects of data reliability, user access, data security, data back-up and user requirements.

We recommended that LCE require the

(a) MA and members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit  the quarterly reports on the utilization of the LGSF, the proof of posting thereof to the Municipality’s website, and in at least three conspicuous places  and other reporting requirements as required under DBM LBC No. 119 and the supporting documents of the progress/final payments made to Contractors; and 

(b) MA to deduct retention money on progress payments to cover uncorrected discovered defects and third-party liabilities, in case the Contractors have not yet been fully paid. Otherwise, require the Contractors concerned to post irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds in favor of the Municipality for the purpose for which the retention money was intended.

We recommended that the LCE instruct 

(a) the MT/Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to submit an inventory tracking report to properly account for all welfare goods procured or received through donations as well as the timing of distribution and recipients; 

(b) Inspectorate Committee and the MGSO to

(i) review the flow of goods, supplies and other inventory to identify weak points in the process and embed control mechanisms to properly track inventory flow; and 

(ii) implement a standardized monitoring system in the various departments handling inventory to have a real time access to information on stocks availability; and 

(c) the concerned personnel to put up a suitable stock room particularly for food items to ensure safety of stocks and avoid undue spoilage.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA, in coordination with the MSWD Officer, to furnish the Audit Team the MOA, liquidation report and payrolls for the first and second tranche for verification and audit.
We recommended that the LCE require the concerned implementing offices to continue being compliant to the rules and regulations on the use of government funds and posting requirements.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) require the MHRMO to submit the (i) Office Order signed by the LCE authorizing the names in the paid payrolls to physically report for work; (ii) documents supporting the number of days worked presented in the payrolls for the payment of COVID-19 Hazard Pay and Special Risk Allowance; and (iii) Daily Time Records, Work Schedule and Assignment certified by the Department Heads; and 

(b) require the MA to closely verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct 

(a) MT to submit in a timely manner the detailed computation for the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts to the MA to ensure correct and up-to-date recording of transactions in the books; and 
(b) MA to track the noted variances to validate whether these were under remittances or unrecorded premiums withheld and take the necessary action, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to

(a) review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and post adjustments, if warranted; and 

(b) strictly comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended that LCE require concerned Municipal Officials to submit the lacking documentary requirements noted in the report and henceforth ensure that transactions of the Municipality would comply with the existing regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a) PMT to 

(i) complete the physical count of all PPE; 
(ii) prepare the required PCs pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and

(iii) provide the MAO with relevant supporting documents to be used as basis in recording the donated parcel of land located in Barangay Inaon; 

(b) MAO to prepare the required PPELCs which shall contain the details of each PPE in the inventory control account in the general ledger; and 

(c) PMT and MAO to reconcile their respective property records regularly for fair presentation of each type of PPE.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to 

(a) include in the GPB the PPAs that are based on gender issues and recommendations in accordance with RA No. 9710; and 

(b) provide a monitoring and evaluation framework for assessing GAD results and outcomes that shall be the basis for strengthening the mainstreaming of GAD programs and activities in the next planning period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)  Municipal Accountant (MA) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to conduct regular reconciliation of their cash records to immediately address the deficiencies thereof; and (b) MA to (i) record and adjust immediately all unrecorded transactions and reconciling items found in their respective records; (ii) prepare bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis to prevent the accumulation of reconciling items that require adjustment in the books to report a reliable Cash-in-Bank account balance; and (iii) recognize in the books the unaccounted five bank accounts based on bank confirmation reply.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of inventories; (ii) recognize in the books the unused inventories amounting to ₱1,189,164.93; and (iii) maintain SLCs to record the acquisition, issuance and disposal of inventories; and (b) Property Officer to prepare the RSMI monthly supported with the RIS for the preparation of Journal Voucher to recognize the issued inventories for the month pursuant to the pertinent Sections of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the LCE require the PMT and MA to (a) complete the physical count of all PPE; (b) prepare the required PCs and PPELCs which shall contain the details of each PPE  in the inventory control account in the general ledger; and (c) reconcile their respective property records regularly for fair presentation of each type of PPE.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and MICTO to reconcile their records to ensure that all Intangible Assets particularly application systems, software, databases and the likes are properly recognized in the books; and (b) MA to make the necessary adjustments and recognize other Intangible Assets previously overlooked in the books.

We recommended that the LCE  (a) create a committee composed of the MA, MTO, MICTO and other concerned Offices/Officers to analyze and make recommendations to address the identified weaknesses in the information technology system; and (b) require the System Provider to make over-all improvements in the System on the aspects of data reliability, user access, data security, data back-up and user requirements.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to submit the DVs and corresponding supporting documents for the transactions totaling to ₱23,590,814.97 for post-audit to preclude the issuance of a notice of suspension on the audit of subject transactions.
We recommended that the LCE require the MA, Appraisal Committee, and Municipal Assessor to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the payment on the procured parcel of land.

We recommended that LCE require the (a) MA and members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit  the quarterly reports on the utilization of the LGSF, the proof of posting thereof to the Municipality’s website, and in at least three conspicuous places  and other reporting requirements as required under DBM LBC No. 119 and the supporting documents of the progress/final payments made to Contractors; and (b) MA to deduct retention money on progress payments to cover uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities, in case the Contractors have not  yet been  fully paid. Otherwise, require the Contractors concerned to post irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds in favor of the Municipality for the purpose for which the retention money was intended.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the BAC and MA to submit the lacking documents to support the procurements made from various suppliers and henceforth, comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the BAC and MA to submit the documentary requirements on the emergency procurement made from various suppliers.

We recommended that the LCE instruct (a) the MT/Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to submit an inventory tracking report to properly account for all welfare goods procured or received through donations as well as the timing of distribution and recipients; (b) Inspectorate Committee and the MGSO to (i) review the flow of goods, supplies and other inventory to identify weak points in the process and embed control mechanisms to properly track inventory flow; and (ii) implement a standardized monitoring system in the various departments handling inventory to have a real time access to information on stocks availability; and (c) the concerned personnel to put up a suitable stock room particularly for food items to ensure safety of stocks and avoid undue spoilage.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA, in coordination with the MSWD Officer, to furnish the Audit Team the MOA, liquidation report and payrolls for the first and second tranche for verification and audit.
We recommended that the LCE require the concerned implementing offices to continue being compliant to the rules and regulations on the use of government funds and posting requirements.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MHRMO to submit the (i) Office Order signed by the LCE authorizing the names in the paid payrolls to physically report for work; (ii) documents supporting the number of days worked presented in the payrolls for the payment of COVID-19 Hazard Pay and Special Risk Allowance; and (iii) Daily Time Records, Work Schedule and Assignment certified by the Department Heads; and (b) require the MA to closely verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct (a) MT to submit in  a timely manner the detailed computation for the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts to the MA to ensure correct and up-to-date recording of transactions in the books; and (b) MA to track the noted variances to validate whether these were under remittances or unrecorded premiums withheld and take the necessary action, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (a) review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/ posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and post adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE (a) activate and strengthen the GFPS in accordance with the afore-cited rules and regulations to ensure the mainstreaming of GAD in the systems and processes of the Municipality; and (b) require the GFPS to (i) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs  is not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes; (ii) update the Municipality’s GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines; and (iii) initiate the capacitation of its members on the use of HGDG tool to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to require all concerned Municipal officials to submit the lacking documentary requirements and henceforth, ensure that transactions of the Municipality would comply with existing laws and regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the monthly submission of reports on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and 

(b) require the MDRRMO to refrain from using the LDRRMF for expenditures which do not directly contribute to the outright avoidance of adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters pursuant to the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1.

We recommended that the LCE through the MENRO  

(a) develop and implement PPAs that will gradually reduce the volume of wastes through recycling composting, and re-use to help reduce expenses for hauling, disposal and transfer of wastes to sanitary landfill; and 

(b) fully utilize the solid waste management fund to identify solutions to effectively comply with RA No. 9003 such as the use of appropriate technology in support to Municipal Ordinances on solid waste avoidance and volume reduction programs.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality; and 

(b) enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided by Section 174 of RA No. 7160 to improve revenue collection.

We recommended that the LCE   

(a) require the HRMO to facilitate the submission of verified DTRs and accomplishment reports to substantiate payment of salaries and allowances of JOs and casual personnel; and 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll at period-end and ensure that DTRs and accomplishment reports are attached thereto to support claims for payment; and 

(c) require the concerned Municipal officials to have the biometric system repaired and maintained to produce an accurate attendance of employees.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and post adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the 

(a) the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to timely submit the detailed computation for the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts to the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure correct and timely recording of transactions in the books; and 

(b) Municipal Accounting Office to track the noted variances to validate whether these were under remittances or unrecorded premiums withheld and take the necessary action, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) PMT to (i) complete the physical count of all PPE; (ii) prepare the required PCs pursuant to the NGAS Manual for LGUs; (iii) provide the MAO with relevant supporting documents to be used as basis in recording the donated parcel of land located in Barangay Inaon; (b) MAO to prepare the required PPELCs which shall contain the details of each PPE  in the inventory control account in the general ledger; and (c) PMT and MAO to reconcile their respective property records regularly for fair presentation of each type of PPE.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office and the BAC to (i) immediately submit the bidding  documents to the Audit Team to preclude the noted transactions from being suspended in audit; (ii) hold the concerned contractors liable for liquidating damages on negative slippage in accordance with Annex D of the CY 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, if warranted; (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) submit a written explanation/justification on the noted observations/deviations on implemented projects; (ii) ensure that the schedule of activities for all construction are in accordance with the detailed engineering designs and programs; (iii) ensure that the necessary documents for civil works in case of temporary work suspensions are prepared and duly approved; (iv) check the progress of the construction at all reasonable times during the project implementation stage to avoid negative slippage; and (v) comply with the standard rates prescribed by DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016 in the preparation of the ABC.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the monthly submission of reports on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and (b) require the MDRRMO to refrain from using the LDRRMF for expenditures which do not directly contribute to the outright avoidance of adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters pursuant to the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1.

We recommended that the LCE through the MENRO  (a) develop and implement PPAs that will gradually reduce the volume of wastes through recycling  composting, and re-use to help reduce expenses for hauling, disposal and transfer of wastes to sanitary landfill; and (b) fully utilize the solid waste management fund to identify solutions to effectively comply with RA No. 9003 such as the use of appropriate technology in support to Municipal Ordinances on solid waste  avoidance and volume reduction programs.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to (a) include in the GPB the PPAs that are based on gender issues and recommendations in accordance with RA No. 9710; and (b) provide a monitoring and evaluation framework for assessing GAD results and outcomes that shall be the basis for strengthening the mainstreaming of GAD programs and activities in the next planning period.

We recommended that the LCE identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided by Section 174 of RA No. 7160 to improve revenue collection.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the HRMO to facilitate the submission of verified DTRs and accomplishment reports to substantiate payment of salaries and allowances of JOs and casual personnel; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll at period-end and ensure that DTRs and accomplishment reports are attached thereto to support claims for payment; and (c) require the concerned Municipal officials to have the biometric system repaired and maintained to produce an accurate attendance of employees.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted posted in the books to trace the noted discrepancy and post adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to timely submit the detailed computation for the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth accounts to the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure correct and timely recording of transactions in the books; and (b) Municipal Accounting Office to track the noted variances to validate whether these were under remittances or unrecorded premiums withheld and take the necessary action, if warranted.

In view of the various lapses noted above, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to 

(a) prioritize the conduct of complete inventory and reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; 

(b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, and record the necessary depreciation expense of all depreciable assets of the Municipality; and 

(c) instruct the Municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality, and verify the properties without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to

a) require the Municipal Planning Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and 

b) direct the concerned officials to strictly observe the general guidelines on the information and publicity of projects undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning & Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office complied and installed proper project signboard in all the project sites since it is a pay item included in the program of works. Sad to say, some residents from the vicinity of the project site took it for their personal purposes. The loss and theft of signboards had always have been a problem of Management ever since. Nevertheless, rest assured that strict compliance and necessary action shall be taken to avoid this kind of circumstances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and to aim further in providing its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to remain compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), and Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000”.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to remain compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), and Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to a) refrain the MSWDO from allocating more than 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment so as not to deprive other functions and concerns of the Municipality of needed funds; and

b) direct the MSWDO to execute the PPAs, monitor the implementation, and to provide documented reports on the impact of the PPAs in order to address the various issues and concerns pertaining to the children’s welfare.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant with the regulations on withholding and remittance of mandatory contributions due to PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG for the employees’ immediate enjoyment of their benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to remain compliant with the regulations on withholding and remittance of mandatory contributions due to PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the full remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the various lapses noted above, we recommended that Management 

a) prioritize the conduct of complete inventory and reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; 

b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively and provide the necessary depreciation on all depreciable assets of the Municipality; and

c) instruct the Municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | Management committed to comply with the recommendations. The Municipal Inventory Team was already created under Office Order No. 2018-19 dated March 19, 2018 to conduct inventory of all PPE of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and

 b) direct the concerned officials to observe strictly the general guidelines on the information and publicity of projects undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the various lapses noted above, we recommended and the   Municipal Mayor agreed to(a) prioritize the conduct of complete inventory and reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; (b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively, and record the necessary depreciation expense of all depreciable assets of the Municipality; and (c) instruct   the Municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality, and verify the properties without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to a) require the Municipal Planning Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and b) direct the concerned officials to strictly observe the    general guidelines on the information and publicity of projects undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant    with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and to aim further in providing its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant    with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR),and Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste    Management Act of 2000”.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to a) refrain the    MSWDO from allocating more than 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment so as not to deprive other functions and concerns of the Municipality of needed funds; and b) direct the MSWDO to execute the PPAs, monitor the implementation, and to     provide documented reports on the impact of the PPAs in order to address the  various issues and concerns pertaining to the children’s welfare.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to a) require the Municipal Budget Officer, together with the focal person and focal departments/offices, to appropriately allot 5% for GAD-related activities in accordance with PCW-NEDA and DBM Joint Memorandum No. 2012-01; b) direct the focal offices to submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report  within five working days from end of January of the ensuing year; and c) require     the focal persons to make a clear assessment of the GAD PPAs for monitoring and evaluation of the GAD implementations.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to remain compliant    with the regulations on withholding and remittance of mandatory contributions due to PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG for the employees’ immediate enjoyment of their benefits.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the full remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit/remit all cash collections intact daily or the next banking day to avoid accumulation of cash in her custody, and possible loss or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | It will be ensured that all collections shall be deposited and or remitted immediately and/or not later than a day after collection.

A memorandum to the collection officers namely Dionisia Esguerra and Remedios Borja was issued requiring them to strictly comply with this policy.

There was a partial deposit of collection on March 31 and June 30, 2017 due to voluminous transactions on March 31, 2017 which fell on a Friday.  Since this was the last day of payment of Real Property Tax, the cashier/collectors have no choice but to accept all payments even if it takes another day or two to finish issuances of Official Receipts to all Property Taxpayers. Same situation happened in depositing all collections to the bank, it also took a day or two. The balance of P58,700.96 and P30,811.58 represent returned checks from the bank after one day and four days, consecutively after validation. The total amount of P4,635,486.27 was properly deposited on the following day but due to some policies/changes implemented by the bank, the depositors were not informed and caught unaware. It took the bank one  to four  days to inform Management  that the checks  deposited were not accepted/validated by their machine resulting from automatic deduction on our deposits on June 30, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Petty Cash Custodian to make replenishments when the disbursements reach at least 75% or as the need requires.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | It is now being implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management

a) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in accordance with the existing regulations and to make the necessary adjustments on reconciling items; and 













b) require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain cashbook on all depository accounts of the Municipality and to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant certified copies of updated Time Deposit balances as basis for updating of records.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | The Municipal Accountant commented to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement as of the end of the year but failed to submit the said report. The Municipal Treasurer already started the preparation of the cashbook for the Cash in Bank account, however, only deposits and check disbursements were recorded. She justified that she could not provide balances at a given date in view of the absence of BRS as her basis for the beginning balance.

All cashbooks in all depository accounts are updated except for cash in bank accounts. This  is due to the daily voluminous check transactions which Management  found  hard  to update  on a regular basis. However, Management assured that best efforts will be exerted and will  do everything to comply with Sec. 26, Volume II of Manual on NGAs.

Management commented to give attention to the said deficiency and directed the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the BRS preparation. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the understatement of P209,654.25 in the Cash in Bank, Time Deposit account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant 

a) to submit the necessary documents relative to the subject purchase of lots for further verification by the Audit Team; and 

b) to attach the required documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 June 14, 2012 in the succeeding disbursements of funds.
observations.managementAction: Management recognized the failure to attach the  necessary document for the purchase of lots. Document are still in process and Management  assured that these documents will be submitted as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation:

(a) require the implementing offices or departments to make appropriate project proposals to fully utilize the idle trust funds pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the respective donors; and

 (b) direct the concerned department to implement proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the return to the concerned NGAs of the unexpended balance of funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 94- 013.

 b) require the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 regarding receipt and utilization of the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY
2017 | The MDRRM officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile their records and that proper adjustment in the books be made. Moreover, the concerned department promised to comply with the regulation concerning LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

a) require the BAC to always adopt competitive bidding before resorting to alternative methods of procurement;

 b) prioritize the establishment of its own Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and in every barangay; and 

c) require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office to monitor the strict compliance on the mandatory segregation of wastes at source.

2017 | Management committed to adhere with the audit recommendation.

 b) direct the MSWDO to execute the PPAs, monitor the implementation, and to provide documented reports on the impact of the PPAs in order to address the various issues and concerns pertaining to the children’s welfare.

AAR CY

We recommended that the Municipality continue to be compliant with the remittances of mandatory contributions due to GSIS, PhilHealth, and PAG-Ibig for the employees to immediately enjoy the benefits from the contributions made.

a) require the Municipal Budget Officer together with the focal person and focal departments/offices to appropriately allot 5% for GAD-related activities in accordance with PCW-NEDA and DBM Joint Memorandum No. 2012-01;

 c) require the focal persons to make a clear assessment of the GAD PPAs for monitoring and evaluation of the GAD implementation.

AAR CY

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management: 

require the BAC to strictly observe the provisions of R.A. 9184  in evaluating the bidding documents and awarding of contracts only to eligible bidders; 









hold the erring contractor responsible for the undelivered services and impose the applicable sanctions to protect the interest of the Municipality;  and

submit justification and basis in hiring the services of another contractor with different terms and without observing the process required by R.A. 9184.

We  recommended  that   Management  require its  concerned officials  to    submit  to the audit team the  required  contracts within  five  (5)  days  after  perfection    and   the  delivery  documents  within  twenty-four  (24)  hours after  acceptance  of  deliveries  of  goods  and  services.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit/remit all cash collections intact daily or the next banking day to avoid accumulation of cash in her custody, and possible loss or misappropriation.
We recommended that Management require the Petty Cash Custodian to make replenishments when the disbursements reach at least 75% or as the need requires.

We recommended that Management a) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in accordance with the existing regulations and to make the necessary adjustments on reconciling items; and b) require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain cashbook on all depository accounts of the Municipality and to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant certified copies of updated Time Deposit balances as basis for updating of records.

We recommended that Management a) prioritize the conduct of complete inventory and reconciliation of the results thereof with the accounting records; b) require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office/GSO to maintain the appropriate subsidiary property records such as PPELC and PLC, respectively and provide the necessary depreciation on all depreciable assets of the Municipality; and c) instruct the Municipal officials concerned to exert effort to facilitate the retrieval or reproduction of the titles on all lots owned by the Municipality and verify property without TCTs and Deeds of Sale with the Register of Deeds (RD).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant a) to submit the necessary documents relative to the subject purchase of lots for further verification by the Audit Team; and b) to attach the required documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 June 14, 2012 in the succeeding disbursements of funds.

We recommended that the Management (a) require the implementing offices or departments to make appropriate project proposals to fully utilize the idle trust funds pursuant to the law governing said grant and/or the Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the respective donors; and (b) direct the concerned department to implement proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the return to the concerned NGAs of the unexpended balance of funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 94- 013.

We recommended that Management a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and b) direct the concerned officials to observe strictly the general guidelines on the information and publicity of projects undertaken by the Municipality in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management a) compel the Municipal Accountant to trace all purchases out of the fund and reconcile with the actual inventory of the MDRRM Office for proper adjustments in the books; and b) require the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 regarding receipt and utilization of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management a) require the BAC to always adopt  competitive bidding before resorting to alternative methods of procurement; b) prioritize the establishment of its own Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and in every barangay; and c) require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office to monitor the strict compliance on the mandatory segregation of wastes at source.

We recommended that Management a) require the MSWDO to fully allocate 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment to fully strengthen and implement the programs, projects and activities of the LCPC; and b) direct the MSWDO to execute the PPAs, monitor the implementation, and to provide documented reports on the impact of the PPAs in order to address the various issues and concerns pertaining to the children’s welfare.

We recommended that Management require the refund of the incentives paid to local barangay officials, otherwise, the concerned department heads shall be made liable for approving the disbursement claims.

observations.managementAction: The Management already adhered and strictly implemented the regulations on the granting and utilization of cash advances.
Refunded the amount of ₱90,000.00 relative to the increase of Ms. Maria Concepcion Imbing.

Moreover, the Management is encouraged to continually comply to accomplish all its developmental projects in accordance with the AIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management undertake the following courses of action:

strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity on programs, projects, and activities of the Municipality; and

properly plan all purchases taking into consideration the timing, quantity, and quality of procured supplies to prevent wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, no purchases of immoderate quantity were made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to transfer the balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002;

require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the inventory and monitoring of all procured and donated supplies, materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and



c. 	direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer already transferred the balance of the LDRRMF of ₱25,652,698.93 to Trust Fund LBP Account No. 3852-1000-00 on January 17, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer complied with the recommendation on the charging of eligible items against the LDRRMF.

The 10-year solid waste management plan was already formulated and submitted for review of the Environment Management Bureau and National Solid Waste Management Commissionon June 9, 2017 for their review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due on claims and compensation of employees and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the remittance of Due to GSIS within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officers and employees to settle their accountabilities and instruct them to adhere strictly to applicable laws, rules and regulation affecting cash advances. See to it that cash advances are granted only to appointed or designated disbursing officers.

We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the criteria on disaster readiness to prevent loss of lives and properties arising from natural disasters or calamities and aim further to equip its MDRRMC personnel with advanced training on disaster preparedness and rescue operations.

We recommended that Management (a) provide adequate and realistic budget for its GAD Plan; (b) require the preparation of a GAD Plan that would directly benefit the women sector, as encouraged in the Philippine Plan for Gender and Development 1995-2025; (c) establish sex disaggregated data to institutionalize and improve GAD planning and budgeting; and (d) enact a GAD Code and implement comprehensive and sustainable GAD programs.

We recommended that the Management:
require the BAC to strictly observe the provisions of R.A. 9184  in evaluating the bidding documents and awarding of contracts only to eligible bidders;
hold the erring contractor responsible for the undelivered services and impose the applicable sanctions to protect the interest of the Municipality;  and
submit justification and basis in hiring the services of another contractor with different terms and without observing the process required by R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to incorporate in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan only eligible projects in accordance with the DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011–1 dated April 11, 2011.  Moreover, the Management is encouraged to continually comply to accomplish all its developmental projects in accordance with the AIP.

We recommended that Management undertake the following courses of action:
strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity on programs, projects, and activities of the Municipality; and
properly plan all purchases taking into consideration the timing, quantity, and quality of procured supplies to prevent wastage of government funds.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to transfer the balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002;
require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the inventory and monitoring of all procured and donated supplies, materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
c.   direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies, medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the Fund following the guidelines under the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.

We  recommended  that   Management  require its  concerned officials  to    submit  to the audit team the  required  contracts within  five  (5)  days  after  perfection    and   the  delivery  documents  within  twenty-four  (24)  hours after  acceptance  of  deliveries  of  goods  and  services.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due on claims and compensation of employees and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with the remittance of Due to GSIS within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management comply strictly with the DOF regulation and secure authority therefrom should the Municipality retain its deposits with other banks.

We recommended that Management direct (a) the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the appropriate offices for the titling of the still untitled lots owned by the Municipality to establish ownership and prevent third-party claims; (b) The Municipal legal officer to declare the six missing titles as “LOST TITLES” and to attend all the hearings relative to the issuance of six new titles; (c)  the Inventory Team reconcile annual physical count of all property, plant and equipment with accounting records; (d) the Accounting Office and the GSO to establish PPELCs and PCs, respectively, starting with current acquisitions until all pertinent years shall have been covered, and maintain them on an up-to-date basis;  and (e) GSO to dispose of all unserviceable assets and waste materials in accordance with regulations.

We recommended that the Local School Board and the Municipal Government utilize the SEF in consonance with the laws and regulations prescribing the activities for the priority spending to attain its objectives and to take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to: (a)see to it that all issued property procured from SEF are covered with ARE and (b) make proper coordination with the Property Officer of the DepEd to strengthen the controls on property accountability to monitor the clearances of those DepEd personnel who are retiring, resigning or terminating their employments.

We recommended that Management (a) continue being complaint with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 (b) involve closely the remaining fifteen barangay officials in the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of wastes at source; and (c) encourage participation of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign in the proper segregation of wastes.
We recommended that the agency continue to strictly adhere to the provisions stated in the code on the collection of real property taxes including the corresponding discounts and interests.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be implemented by the Municipality. See to it that the projects to be undertaken are within the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Government refrain from charging to their funds those membership dues and similar fees of their officers and employees paid to their respective associations or organizations which are not in conformity with the existing regulations.

It is recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan enact resolution adjusting/ revising the Market Code to be at par with the nearby municipalities in order to increase revenue and to improve delivery of basic services to the general public.
We recommend that management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.
